sorting-direction.label: Sorting Direction (ASC, DESC)
sorting-direction.summary: Control sort order with ASC and DESC keywords.

sk1.title: Sorting Results in SQL
sk1.bodyMarkdown:
In SQL, you can control the order of your query results using the `ORDER BY` clause. By default, results are sorted in ascending order (from smallest to largest or A to Z). You can explicitly specify the direction using the `ASC` (ascending) or `DESC` (descending) keywords.

For example, to list all products from the `products` table by price from lowest to highest:

```sql
SELECT name, price FROM products ORDER BY price ASC;
```

To list products from highest to lowest price:

```sql
SELECT name, price FROM products ORDER BY price DESC;
```

You can also sort by multiple columns, specifying the direction for each:

```sql
SELECT name, category, price FROM products ORDER BY category ASC, price DESC;
```
This sorts first by category (A-Z), then by price within each category (highest to lowest).

q1.title: Default Sorting Direction
q1.prompt: What is the default sorting direction when you use ORDER BY in SQL without specifying ASC or DESC?
q1.hint: Think about what happens if you just use ORDER BY column_name.

help.concept: ORDER BY sorts results in ascending order by default unless DESC is specified.
help.hint_1: ORDER BY column sorts from lowest to highest values.
help.hint_2: If you want descending order, you must explicitly use DESC.

q2.title: Recognizing ASC and DESC Usage
q2.prompt: Which of the following SQL queries will return the product names sorted from most expensive to least expensive?
q2.hint: Look for ORDER BY with DESC on the price column.

help.concept: DESC sorts values in descending order, which is needed for most expensive to least expensive.
help.hint_1: ASC is for lowest to highest; DESC is for highest to lowest.
help.hint_2: Only the query with ORDER BY price DESC will sort from most to least expensive.

options.a: SELECT name FROM products ORDER BY price DESC;
options.b: SELECT name FROM products ORDER BY price ASC;
options.c: SELECT name FROM products ORDER BY price;
options.d: SELECT name FROM products ORDER BY name DESC;

q3.title: Sorting Steps in SQL
q3.prompt: Arrange the steps to sort products by stock in descending order.
q3.hint: Think about the SQL clause order and keywords.

help.concept: The ORDER BY clause comes after SELECT, and DESC follows the column name to indicate descending order.
help.hint_1: ORDER BY comes after the SELECT statement.
help.hint_2: DESC must follow the column name in ORDER BY to sort in descending order.

q4.title: Fill in the Sorting Direction
q4.prompt: Complete the query to list all product names and prices from the products table, sorted by price from lowest to highest.

help.concept: the missing term is used to sort results in ascending order, which is from lowest to highest.
help.hint_1: Ascending order is the default, but you can specify it explicitly with the missing term.
help.hint_2: Use the missing term to sort from lowest to highest values.

q4.template: SELECT name, price FROM products ORDER BY price [___];

q5.title: Sort by Multiple Columns
q5.prompt: Write a SQL query to list all products (name, category, price) sorted first by category (A-Z), then by price from highest to lowest within each category.
q5.hint: Use ORDER BY with two columns and specify DESC for price.

help.concept: ORDER BY can sort by multiple columns, each with its own direction. ASC sorts ascending, DESC sorts descending.
help.hint_1: List category first in ORDER BY, then price with DESC.
help.hint_2: ORDER BY category ASC, price DESC will sort as required.
